old_claim.note: Philosophy provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained because the source-backed algebra chronology predates the graph philosophy node by more than a millennium.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica Algebra history section anchors Babylonian equation-solving traditions around 1800 BCE, before the graph philosophy node.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Basic algebraic equation solving is source-anchored in Old Babylonian mathematics rather than Greek philosophical traditions.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Philosophy may later shape mathematical proof traditions, but it is not a prerequisite for Old Babylonian equation-solving practice.

ontology_before: Basic Algebra treated Philosophy as an enabling prerequisite.
ontology_after: Basic Algebra remains connected to mathematics and writing, without depending on later philosophy.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: Basic Algebra must not directly depend on Philosophy unless the node is narrowed to philosophical/proof-oriented algebra.
would_reject_if: The Basic Algebra node is re-scoped to Greek philosophical mathematics rather than equation-solving methods.
The philosophy node is re-scoped and redated to an older Mesopotamian abstraction tradition that directly supports algebra.
A stronger source shows the Old Babylonian evidence should not be treated as basic algebraic equation solving.
short_reason: Old Babylonian algebraic practice predates the philosophy node.
